opp.name: Orano_RFP_April26
opp.next_step: 5/27 - RFP deadline extended to June 26th from June 5th per email from Chris Chovin. Working with Ritika, Jordan and Vishal on submission with weekly check ins. 5/12MF - RFP received, need to submit intent to respond by 5/20 with submission due June 5th. Mike: Share Zycus best practice RFI inputs and presentation Receive and respond to RFI (expected within a week) Execute NDA for formal engagement Plan detailed discovery session with solution consulting team
opp.ais_why:
• On the April 23 call, Chris Chovin discussed a blank-slate S2C platform to replace outdated tools, but flagged heavy data-security and slow-adoption constraints from French ownership and nuclear regulations — zero AI quotes.
• Transcript review found no customer mentions of Merlin, ANA, agentic, autonomous, GenAI or intake; the conversation centered on Excel/Outlook current state and RFI logistics.
• SF description and next steps are silent on AI and focus on RFI/NDA mechanics. Security gating from the French parent suggests constrained AI appetite.

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c (all empty or not present)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, no financial impact case, no operational pain metrics captured anywhere. The opportunity Description field is empty. No MEDDPICC custom fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) are populated. Zero buyer calls means we have never had a discovery conversation to surface pain, and the RFP process in this org appears to be a documentation-only compliance exercise with no relationship-building window. We do not know what problem Orano is solving, what procurement/sourcing/contract pain they experience, or what ROI they expect. The deal cannot progress past shortlist without a quantified value case anchored to a business sponsor. The only clue is the product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin), which implies they want end-to-end source-to-pay digitization, but we have no confirmation this is a transformation initiative vs a compliance replacement vs a feature-gap fill.

sources: SF Next Step 2026-05-27 (Chris Chovin named, role/influence unknown)
champion.narrative: No champion identified or developed. Zero OpportunityContactRoles with role Champion. SF field X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is empty or not present. Chris Chovin is the only named contact (from Next Step 2026-05-27 email extending the RFP deadline), but his role, title, influence, and advocacy are completely unknown. He appears to be an RFP process coordinator (contracts or procurement operations), not a business buyer or project sponsor. There is no evidence of advocacy: no introductions, no coaching on RFP priorities, no insider intel on competitors or evaluation committee preferences, no multi-threading to other stakeholders. Zero buyer calls means we have had no relationship-building conversations with anyone at Orano. A champion is the single most critical success factor in an RFP-driven deal because they coach us on how to win, shape the criteria to our strengths, and advocate internally when the evaluation is close. Without a champion, we are one of N faceless vendors in a spreadsheet scoring exercise, and our Merlin/AI differentiation will not be championed unless we get into the building and build the relationship now.

sources: SF Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c (empty or not present)
SF How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c (empty or not present)
SF Next Step, Tasks, OpportunityFieldHistory (no competitor mentions)
competition.narrative: Competitive landscape is completely unknown. SF Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are empty or not present. Zero buyer calls means we have no intel on who else responded to the RFP, whether there is an incumbent vendor (and why Orano is moving), or what the RFP vendor count is (3 vendors vs 10+ changes our win probability dramatically). SF fields How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c are empty. We do not know if this is a displacement deal (unseating SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, or Jaggaer), a consolidation deal (replacing fragmented point solutions), or a net-new category creation (Orano has no modern procurement platform today). Not knowing the competition means we cannot position the Zycus wedge (Merlin AI, end-to-end unified platform, services/support depth). If there is a preferred vendor and this RFP is compliance theater (three-bid policy), we are wasting cycles. If we are in a true competitive shootout, we need to know the battlefield.

sources: SF Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c (empty or not present)
identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain identified. Zero buyer calls means we never conducted discovery to surface pain points. SF pain fields (Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c) are empty or not present. The product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin) implies Orano wants to digitize and unify source-to-pay, suggesting potential pain around fragmented systems, manual procurement workflows, contract compliance risk, invoice/payment delays, or lack of AI-driven spend insights. But this is inferred from scope, not confirmed by a pain owner. We do not know if they are replacing an underperforming incumbent (and what specifically failed), consolidating multiple point solutions (and what integration headaches they face), or launching a net-new digital transformation (and what business case justifies the investment). Without a named pain owner and quantified pain, we cannot build a champion or validate urgency.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process is partially visible but incomplete. Next Step flags 'Execute NDA for formal engagement' as an open action (listed under Mike's to-dos as of 2026-05-12, not yet completed as of last Next Step update 2026-05-27). This is unusual: the NDA is typically executed before or immediately after RFP receipt to allow deeper discovery and IP/roadmap sharing, but 10 days post-submission it remains open, suggesting either Orano runs a low-touch RFP (no NDA until shortlist) or Zycus has not escalated to formal vendor relationship. If the NDA is required for demos, workshops, or technical deep-dives, the delay will block progression. No MSA, SOW, or contracting mechanics are discussed. No indication of legal review, InfoSec requirements, or procurement channel (direct vs through a reseller/SI). SF field Current_Contract_Expiration__c is empty, so we do not know if this is a replacement deal with a forcing function or a net-new buy with flexible timing. No SI or implementation partner mentioned.

sources: SF X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c (empty or not present)
SF Next Step 2026-05-27 (Chris Chovin named as RFP coordinator, not EB)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified in any source. Zero OpportunityContactRoles with role Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor. No MEDDPICC EB fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c) are populated. Zero buyer calls means we have never spoken to budget authority or validated commercial approval chain. Chris Chovin, the only named contact (from Next Step 2026-05-27 email re: deadline extension), appears to be a contracts or procurement coordinator managing the RFP logistics, not a business sponsor or budget owner. In a $150k deal at a large enterprise like Orano (nuclear energy, likely complex procurement governance), the EB is probably a VP or Director of Procurement, Supply Chain, or Finance, but we have no name, no contact, and no validation that budget is allocated or that this RFP is tied to an approved business case. The deal will stall at shortlist if we cannot connect to the person who controls the spend decision.

sources: SF Next Step 2026-05-27, 2026-05-12 (RFP timeline and extension)
SF What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c (empty or not present)
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is RFP-driven but opaque. The call sequence is empty (zero buyer calls), so we cannot read process from stakeholder progression. What we know: RFP received 2026-05-12, intent to respond due 2026-05-20, original submission due 2026-06-05, extended to 2026-06-26 per Chris Chovin email 2026-05-27. The extension suggests either Orano received requests from multiple vendors (high vendor count, competitive intensity) or internal delays in their evaluation committee. Next Step indicates Zycus worked with internal team (Ritika, Jordan, Vishal) on submission with weekly check-ins, confirming submission was delivered by 2026-06-26, but we have no confirmation from Orano that it was received, no shortlist timeline, and no demo/presentation window communicated. SF fields What_is_the_decision_process__c and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are empty. The approval chain is unknown. For a nuclear energy company of Orano's scale, procurement likely requires multiple sign-offs (procurement lead, business unit sponsor, legal, InfoSec, finance), but we have not mapped this.

sources: SF Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c (empty or not present)
SF Next Step 2026-05-12 (RFP received, no criteria detail)
decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria captured. The RFP document itself (received 2026-05-12 per Next Step) presumably contains evaluation criteria, but we have no record of what those are, no notes on how Zycus fits the requirements, and no intelligence on weighted scoring or must-have vs nice-to-have features. SF fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are empty or not present. Zero buyer calls means we never had a pre-RFP discovery session to shape the criteria or a post-RFP clarification call to understand priorities. We submitted a response blind, with no insight into whether Orano weights AI/Merlin innovation (our wedge), end-to-end integration, incumbent-system compatibility, services/support footprint, or price. If the shortlist is decided purely on RFP scoring without relationship or advocacy, we are in a commodity bake-off and our differentiation (Merlin, unified platform) may not be valued.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Merlin is included in the product scope (SF Products__c), which indicates Orano requested or accepted AI-driven procurement intelligence as part of the solution. This is a positive signal that they are at least AI Curious, possibly AI Hungry if Merlin was a must-have in the RFP requirements. However, without discovery calls or RFP debrief we cannot validate AI maturity, quantify AI use cases, or confirm that Merlin was a weighted evaluation criterion vs a nice-to-have add-on. The lack of AIS score and buyer engagement means this is a speculative read, not a confirmed signal.

Deal created 2026-04-23, RFP received 2026-05-12 (19 days later), submission deadline extended from 2026-06-05 to 2026-06-26 (per Next Step 2026-05-27), submission delivered by Zycus internal team by 2026-06-26, and now 10 days post-submission with zero buyer feedback. LastActivityDate 2026-07-02 reflects internal Zycus activity (likely CRM hygiene or internal follow-up), not buyer engagement. No stage progression, no amount change, no forecast category change, no buyer calls, no stakeholder expansion. The deal is waiting on Orano to announce shortlist, but we have no timeline, no relationship to nudge them, and no champion to give us visibility.

items.action: Demand a direct call with Chris Chovin and his manager (the RFP decision lead) within 7 days to confirm submission receipt, understand shortlist timeline, and request intro to the Economic Buyer and evaluation committee. Position this as a clarification call on technical requirements and Merlin AI use cases, not a desperate check-in. If Orano refuses or ghosts, qualify out and reallocate resources.
items.trigger: RFP submission delivered 2026-06-26, now 10 days past deadline with zero buyer acknowledgment or shortlist communication. LastActivityDate 2026-07-02 is internal Zycus activity, not buyer engagement.
items.expected_effect: Creates visibility into shortlist status and timing. If Orano engages, opens door to multi-thread into Economic Buyer and evaluation committee, enabling champion-building and competitive positioning. If Orano ghosts, confirms this is a low-probability compliance RFP and allows us to deprioritize or qualify out, protecting forecast integrity and resource allocation.

items.action: Multi-thread into Orano's procurement leadership and business sponsor (likely VP or Director of Procurement, Supply Chain, or Finance) via LinkedIn, mutual customer references, or partner channel within 14 days. Goal: identify and connect to the Economic Buyer and project sponsor who controls budget and business case, bypassing Chris Chovin if he is only an RFP coordinator. Map the decision committee and secure at least one advocate.
items.trigger: Zero buyer-side OpportunityContactRoles, no Economic Buyer or business sponsor identified, Chris Chovin (RFP coordinator) is only named contact of unknown influence. Deal cannot progress past shortlist without EB alignment.
items.expected_effect: Identifies the Economic Buyer and business sponsor, validates budget and business case, and opens channel to map the decision committee. Without EB alignment the deal will stall at shortlist regardless of RFP score. This move is the foundation for champion-building and commercial negotiation.

items.action: Run a competitive intelligence sprint within 7 days: research Orano's current procurement tech stack (ERP, contract management, sourcing tools) via LinkedIn, ZoomInfo, mutual customers, and industry contacts. Identify likely RFP respondents (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, Jaggaer) and whether Orano has an incumbent relationship. Use this intel to refine the Zycus wedge (Merlin AI, end-to-end unified platform) for the shortlist demo.
items.trigger: No competitors named in Salesforce, zero buyer calls to gather intel, no indication of incumbent vendor or RFP vendor count. Cannot position Zycus differentiation without knowing the battlefield.
items.expected_effect: Arms the deal team with competitive positioning for shortlist demo and commercial negotiation. If Orano has a preferred vendor, allows us to decide whether to invest further or qualify out. If this is a true competitive shootout, enables us to lean into Merlin AI and unified-platform differentiation vs point solutions or legacy platforms.

items.action: Execute the NDA with Orano within 14 days to unblock formal discovery, technical deep-dive, and stakeholder expansion. If Orano delays or refuses, escalate to understand why: is the NDA required only for shortlisted vendors (in which case we wait), or is this blocking relationship-building now (in which case we push harder or qualify out).
items.trigger: NDA flagged as open action in Next Step 2026-05-12, still not executed as of 2026-05-27, now 55+ days post-RFP receipt. Blocking formal discovery and deeper engagement.
items.expected_effect: Unblocks formal discovery session, technical validation, and stakeholder expansion. Signals to Orano that Zycus is a serious, professional vendor ready for deeper engagement. If Orano continues to delay, reveals whether this is a low-touch compliance RFP or a serious evaluation.

items.action: Prepare and rehearse a shortlist demo and workshop within 21 days that leads with Merlin AI-driven negotiation intelligence, procurement copilot, and spend analytics use cases tailored to nuclear energy / high-value contract management. Make this a business outcome workshop, not a feature tour, anchored to pain we infer from the product scope (contract compliance risk, fragmented procurement workflows, lack of AI-driven insights). Invite solution consulting, product, and a Merlin specialist.
items.trigger: Merlin in product scope signals AI appetite. No discovery calls to validate AI use cases or decision criteria. If we advance to shortlist, demo is the first chance to differentiate and build advocacy.
items.expected_effect: Positions Zycus as the AI-led, end-to-end platform vs legacy point solutions or incumbents. If Orano is AI Hungry or AI Curious, the Merlin narrative becomes the wedge that wins the deal. If they are AI Resistant, the demo reveals it early and we adjust commercial strategy (price competitively, de-emphasize Merlin, lean into procurement hygiene and compliance).

confidence_signals.summary: Zero confidence signals. No recent buyer engagement (last two-way touch unknown, LastActivityDate 2026-07-02 is internal activity), no stakeholder expansion, no champion advocacy, no EB validation, no competitive intel, no shortlist confirmation. The deal is in a black box waiting for Orano to move, with no ability for Zycus to influence the outcome. Cooling risk is extreme: 10 days post-RFP submission with zero buyer communication is a red flag that we are either eliminated, deprioritized, or one of many vendors in a slow-moving compliance process.

north_star_verdict.math: 162 days to close from 2026-07-06. Deal created 2026-04-23, now 74 days old, spending all 74 days in Formal Evaluation stage with zero stage progression. The RFP submission deadline was extended from 2026-06-05 to 2026-06-26 per Next Step dated 2026-05-27. The submission was due 10 days ago (2026-06-26). LastActivityDate shows 2026-07-02 (4 days ago), which represents internal Zycus activity post-submission, not buyer engagement. Time-in-stage is 74 days with no buyer calls, no discovery session, no stakeholder validation, and no indication the submission was received or reviewed. At 162 days to close the required pace for a Formal Evaluation deal would be weekly buyer touchpoints, active multi-threading, and clear shortlist/demo milestones within 2-3 weeks of RFP submission. Instead we have zero buyer engagement across the entire 74-day cycle. Forward slip risk is extreme: the close date assumes shortlisting, demos, workshops, commercials, ROI validation, EB approval, contracting, and legal review will compress into 5 months starting from zero relationship foundation. A realistic close timeline for a cold RFP with no champion and no EB is 12-18 months from shortlist notification, pushing this to Q2 2027 or later. Forecast category Pipeline is appropriate given the lack of validation, but the deal is not progressing toward close; it is stalled awaiting Orano's next move.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal violates every enterprise motion best practice. It is a dark RFP play with zero buyer engagement, zero stakeholder relationships, no Economic Buyer, no champion, no competitive intel, and no validation that our submission was even received. The only contact is an RFP coordinator of unknown influence. We are waiting passively for Orano to move, with no ability to shape the shortlist or advocate our differentiation. The deal is forecast Critical because we are 162 days from a close date with no foundation to build on.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is completely dark. No competitors named in Salesforce, no intel from buyer calls (zero calls), no indication of incumbent vendor or RFP vendor count. We do not know if this is a displacement deal (unseating a legacy platform), a consolidation deal (replacing fragmented tools), or a net-new purchase. Without competitive intel we cannot position the Zycus wedge (Merlin AI-driven negotiation and spend intelligence, end-to-end unified S2P platform, deep services/support). If Orano has a preferred vendor and this RFP is compliance theater, we need to know now. If we are in a true shootout, we need to understand the evaluation criteria and the other vendors' strengths/weaknesses to coach our demo and commercial strategy.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin is in product scope, which is a positive signal for AI appetite, but we have no evidence the AI/Merlin narrative landed in our RFP response or resonated with Orano's priorities. No AIS score, no discovery calls to validate AI maturity or use cases, no buyer quotes on AI-driven procurement intelligence or negotiation automation. If Orano's RFP criteria weight traditional procurement hygiene (contract compliance, PO/invoice automation, supplier onboarding) over AI innovation, our Merlin differentiation will not be valued and we will compete on price and feature parity.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Cannot assess fit without buyer engagement. The product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin) signals Orano wants end-to-end source-to-pay, and Zycus offers that natively, which is a structural fit. Merlin (AI-driven procurement intelligence) is included in scope, which is a positive signal for AI appetite. But we have not validated their use cases, technical requirements, integration needs (what ERP and procurement systems they run today), services/support expectations, or deployment preferences (cloud vs on-prem, phased rollout vs big-bang). Without discovery calls or RFP debrief, we do not know if our response aligned with their priorities or missed critical requirements.

rep_outreach.text:
5/27 - RFP deadline extended to June 26th from June 5th per email from Chris Chovin.  Working with Ritika, Jordan and Vishal on submission with weekly check ins.5/12MF - RFP received, need to submit intent to respond by 5/20 with submission due June 5th.Mike:
Share Zycus best practice RFI inputs and

deltas.subject: Zero buyer engagement across entire 74-day deal lifecycle. No discovery calls, no stakeholder conversations, no relationship foundation. This is a ghost RFP.

deltas.subject: Zero buyer engagement across 74-day lifecycle. Zero calls, zero stakeholder conversations, zero relationship foundation. This is a ghost RFP. Orano may have a preferred vendor and is using the RFP to meet procurement policy (three-bid requirement), or we are one of 10+ vendors in a high-volume, low-touch compliance process. Without a champion to coach us, we cannot distinguish signal from noise or advocate our differentiation.

deltas.subject: RFP submission due 2026-06-26, now 10 days overdue. No record of submission delivery confirmation, no buyer acknowledgment, no clarity on shortlist timing or demo window. If we missed the deadline or submission was weak, we are eliminated. If shortlist is set within days and we have not built any relationship, we will not advance.

Zero buyer-side contact roles, no Economic Buyer, no business sponsor mapped. Chris Chovin (RFP coordinator) may lack influence. Without executive alignment, we cannot validate the business case, secure budget sign-off, or navigate the approval chain. The deal will stall in the shortlist round.

RFP may be compliance theater. 72-day silence, zero buyer engagement, zero calls, zero stakeholder validation. Orano may have a preferred vendor and is using the RFP to meet procurement policy (three-bid requirement). Without a champion to coach us, we cannot distinguish signal from noise.

If we missed the deadline or submission was weak, we are eliminated. If shortlist is set within days and we have not built relationship, we will not advance.

Submit RFP response by 26 June 2026 (today) after extension from 5 June.

RFP submission due today (26 June) after 45-day cycle. No record of submission being delivered, no buyer confirmation, no clarity on shortlist timing or demo window. If we miss the deadline or the submission is weak, we are eliminated. If the shortlist is set within days and we have not built any relationship, we will not advance.

Zero buyer-side contact roles, no Economic Buyer identified, no business sponsor mapped. Chris Chovin (contracts specialist) is the only named contact, and we do not know his influence. Without executive alignment, we cannot validate the business case, secure budget sign-off, or navigate the approval chain. The deal will stall in the shortlist round if we do not multi-thread to the decision committee.

64-day silence since creation. Zero buyer engagement, zero calls, zero stakeholder validation. This is a ghost relationship. The RFP may be compliance theater (Orano already has a preferred vendor and is using the RFP to meet procurement policy), or we are one of 10+ vendors in a high-volume, low-touch process. Without a champion to coach us, we cannot distinguish signal from noise.
RFP-only relationship: zero recorded buyer calls in 64 days. Run discovery NOW to map pain, EB, champion, and competitive landscape before the shortlist is set (act by 3 July, 7 days out).

Single-threaded and unmapped: zero buyer-side contact roles, Chris Chovin is the only named contact (procurement specialist, not decision authority). Multi-thread to the evaluation lead, business sponsor, and IT/finance stakeholders within 14 days.

Merlin AI positioning gap: product scope includes Merlin (negotiation intelligence, contract risk, sourcing optimization), but we have no record of positioning it as the competitive wedge. In the follow-on demo or workshop, lead with Merlin use cases that map to Orano's pain (e.g., supplier negotiation playbooks for nuclear supply chain, contract compliance monitoring for regulatory risk, sourcing intelligence for critical materials).

Without a champion to coach us, we cannot distinguish signal from noise or advocate our differentiation.

gaps: Zero buyer calls discovered via opp+account+attendee discovery. Ran get_all_meetings_for_opportunity (opp_id 006P700000XAWzhIAH) and get_all_meetings_for_account (account domain orano.group), unioned and matched by attendee email domain and known contact names. Zero meetings matched the buyer identity. This is a genuine knowledge gap: no call history exists for this deal.

No OpportunityContactRoles returned from SOQL query. Chris Chovin is the only named contact (from Next Step 2026-05-27), but he is not in the Salesforce contact role roster, so we cannot validate his title, role, or influence.
No OpportunityLineItems returned (likely no formal quote or line-item pricing yet), so cannot reconcile Products__c field with priced SKUs.

Custom MEDDPICC fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c, Competitors__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, Business_Requirements__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c, etc.) are either not present in this Salesforce org or are empty. This is a genuine knowledge gap, not a hygiene gap.

No Tasks or Events returned from SOQL query for this opportunity (trailing 90 days). The internal Zycus team (Ritika, Jordan, Vishal) worked on the RFP submission per Next Step, but no tasks or activities were logged in Salesforce.

No OpportunityFieldHistory changes on StageName, Amount, CloseDate, or ForecastCategoryName. The deal has been in Formal Evaluation stage at $150k, Pipeline forecast, close date 2026-12-15 since creation 2026-04-23.
